items.final_wording: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
source.population: People aged 16 years and over with suspected sepsis, excluding people who are or have recently been pregnant.
source.setting: Community, ambulance, acute hospital, and emergency assessment settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for suspected sepsis recognition and assessment in non-pregnant people aged 16 years and over; partial for a broad fever or suspected-infection workflow and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Fever in Children
source.population: Children with fever.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for pediatric fever telehealth documentation; partial for a mixed-age fever/URTI workflow.

exact_location.heading: 5.1 Virtual Clinical Assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.2 — recognition, suspected infection, source, and risk factors

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.

items.rationale: The exact NICE suspected-sepsis recognition section supports documenting onset, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and clinician risk context when infection or sepsis is clinically considered; it does not equate fever with sepsis.

items.final_wording: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
source.population: People aged 16 years and over with suspected sepsis, excluding people who are or have recently been pregnant.
source.setting: Community, ambulance, acute hospital, and emergency assessment settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for suspected sepsis recognition and assessment in non-pregnant people aged 16 years and over; partial for a broad fever or suspected-infection workflow and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendation 1.3 — history, observations, circulation, respiration, skin, urine output, and source examination

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

items.rationale: The exact assessment section supports measured vital data, focused assessment, illness severity and general appearance only when assessed.

items.final_wording: Supports confusion, respiratory compromise, hypotension, tachycardia, oliguria, temperature abnormality, non-blanching or mottled skin, and immunosuppression as assessed risk features.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
source.population: People aged 16 years and over with suspected sepsis, excluding people who are or have recently been pregnant.
source.setting: Community, ambulance, acute hospital, and emergency assessment settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for suspected sepsis recognition and assessment in non-pregnant people aged 16 years and over; partial for a broad fever or suspected-infection workflow and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.5–1.6 — community and acute risk criteria

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports confusion, respiratory compromise, hypotension, tachycardia, oliguria, temperature abnormality, non-blanching or mottled skin, and immunosuppression as assessed risk features.

items.rationale: The exact risk-criteria section supports recording time-critical symptoms or abnormal observations only when clinician-assessed; it does not generate a risk classification.

items.final_wording: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Fever in Children

source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for pediatric fever telehealth documentation; partial for a mixed-age fever/URTI workflow.

exact_location.heading: 5.1 Virtual Clinical Assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.

items.rationale: The exact DHA pediatric fever assessment section independently supports severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and appearance when assessing a child; it is not generalized to adults.

items.final_wording: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Fever in Children
source.population: Children with fever.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for pediatric fever telehealth documentation; partial for a mixed-age fever/URTI workflow.

exact_location.heading: 7. Referral Criteria

source.evidence_paraphrase: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.

items.rationale: The exact pediatric referral section supports documenting escalation or handover only when clinician-decided for a child.

added_items.final_wording: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
source.population: People aged 16 years and over with suspected sepsis, excluding people who are or have recently been pregnant.
source.setting: Community, ambulance, acute hospital, and emergency assessment settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for suspected sepsis recognition and assessment in non-pregnant people aged 16 years and over; partial for a broad fever or suspected-infection workflow and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.2 — recognition, suspected infection, source, and risk factors

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.

added_items.rationale: The exact NICE suspected-sepsis recognition section supports documenting onset, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and clinician risk context when infection or sepsis is clinically considered; it does not equate fever with sepsis.

added_items.final_wording: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
source.population: People aged 16 years and over with suspected sepsis, excluding people who are or have recently been pregnant.
source.setting: Community, ambulance, acute hospital, and emergency assessment settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for suspected sepsis recognition and assessment in non-pregnant people aged 16 years and over; partial for a broad fever or suspected-infection workflow and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendation 1.3 — history, observations, circulation, respiration, skin, urine output, and source examination

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

added_items.rationale: The exact assessment section supports measured vital data, focused assessment, illness severity and general appearance only when assessed.

added_items.final_wording: Supports confusion, respiratory compromise, hypotension, tachycardia, oliguria, temperature abnormality, non-blanching or mottled skin, and immunosuppression as assessed risk features.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
source.population: People aged 16 years and over with suspected sepsis, excluding people who are or have recently been pregnant.
source.setting: Community, ambulance, acute hospital, and emergency assessment settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for suspected sepsis recognition and assessment in non-pregnant people aged 16 years and over; partial for a broad fever or suspected-infection workflow and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.5–1.6 — community and acute risk criteria

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports confusion, respiratory compromise, hypotension, tachycardia, oliguria, temperature abnormality, non-blanching or mottled skin, and immunosuppression as assessed risk features.

added_items.rationale: The exact risk-criteria section supports recording time-critical symptoms or abnormal observations only when clinician-assessed; it does not generate a risk classification.

added_items.final_wording: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Fever in Children
source.population: Children with fever.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for pediatric fever telehealth documentation; partial for a mixed-age fever/URTI workflow.

exact_location.heading: 5.1 Virtual Clinical Assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.

added_items.rationale: The exact DHA pediatric fever assessment section independently supports severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and appearance when assessing a child; it is not generalized to adults.

added_items.final_wording: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Fever in Children
source.population: Children with fever.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for pediatric fever telehealth documentation; partial for a mixed-age fever/URTI workflow.

exact_location.heading: 7. Referral Criteria

source.evidence_paraphrase: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.

added_items.rationale: The exact pediatric referral section supports documenting escalation or handover only when clinician-decided for a child.
